authors_text: Jialiang Sun,Wen Yao,Tingsong Jiang,Chao Li,Xiaoqian Chen
title: $A^{3}D$: A Platform of Searching for Robust Neural Architectures and Efficient Adversarial Attacks
title_zh: $A^{3}D$：寻找鲁棒神经架构和高效对抗攻击的平台
abstract: The robustness of deep neural networks (DNN) models has attracted increasing attention due to the urgent need for security in many applications. Numerous existing open-sourced tools or platforms are developed to evaluate the robustness of DNN models by ensembling the majority of adversarial attack or defense algorithms. Unfortunately, current platforms do not possess the ability to optimize the architectures of DNN models or the configuration of adversarial attacks to further enhance the robustness of models or the performance of adversarial attacks. To alleviate these problems, in this paper, we first propose a novel platform called auto adversarial attack and defense ($A^{3}D$), which can help search for robust neural network architectures and efficient adversarial attacks. In $A^{3}D$, we employ multiple neural architecture search methods, which consider different robustness evaluation metrics, including four types of noises: adversarial noise, natural noise, system noise, and quantified metrics, resulting in finding robust architectures. Besides, we propose a mathematical model for auto adversarial attack, and provide multiple optimization algorithms to search for efficient adversarial attacks. In addition, we combine auto adversarial attack and defense together to form a unified framework. Among auto adversarial defense, the searched efficient attack can be used as the new robustness evaluation to further enhance the robustness. In auto adversarial attack, the searched robust architectures can be utilized as the threat model to help find stronger adversarial attacks. Experiments on CIFAR10, CIFAR100, and ImageNet datasets demonstrate the feasibility and effectiveness of the proposed platform, which can also provide a benchmark and toolkit for researchers in the application of automated machine learning in evaluating and improving the DNN model robustnesses.

In $A^{3}D$, we employ multiple neural architecture search methods, which consider different robustness evaluation metrics, including four types of noises: adversarial noise, natural noise, system noise, and quantified metrics, resulting in finding robust architectures. abstract_zh: 由于许多应用对安全性的迫切需求，深度神经网络（DNN）模型的鲁棒性引起了越来越多的关注。许多现有的开源工具或平台被开发出来，通过集成大多数对抗性攻击或防御算法来评估 DNN 模型的鲁棒性。不幸的是，当前平台不具备优化DNN模型架构或对抗性攻击配置的能力，以进一步增强模型的鲁棒性或对抗性攻击的性能。为了缓解这些问题，在本文中，我们首先提出了一种称为自动对抗攻击和防御（$A^{3}D$）的新颖平台，它可以帮助搜索鲁棒的神经网络架构和有效的对抗攻击。在$A^{3}D$中，我们采用多种神经架构搜索方法，考虑不同的稳健性评估指标，包括四种类型的噪声：对抗性噪声、自然噪声、系统噪声和量化指标，从而找到稳健的架构。此外，我们提出了自动对抗攻击的数学模型，并提供多种优化算法来搜索有效的对抗攻击。此外，我们将自动对抗攻击和防御结合在一起，形成一个统一的框架。在自动对抗防御中，搜索到的有效攻击可以作为新的鲁棒性评估，进一步增强鲁棒性。在自动对抗攻击中，搜索到的鲁棒架构可以用作威胁模型，帮助发现更强的对抗攻击。在CIFAR10、CIFAR100和ImageNet数据集上的实验证明了该平台的可行性和有效性，也可以为研究人员应用自动化机器学习评估和提高DNN模型鲁棒性提供基准和工具包。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一个名为A³D的平台，用于搜索鲁棒神经网络架构和高效对抗攻击方法。
AI.motivation: 现有平台无法优化神经网络架构或对抗攻击配置，导致模型鲁棒性评估和攻击效率受限。
AI.method: 结合多种神经架构搜索方法与自动对抗攻击数学模型，通过四类噪声指标搜索鲁棒架构，并利用优化算法提升攻击效率，形成攻防统一框架。
AI.result: 在CIFAR10、CIFAR100和ImageNet数据集上验证了平台有效性，提供了自动化机器学习的基准工具。
AI.conclusion: A³D平台有效提升了模型鲁棒性与攻击效率，为相关研究提供了实用工具和基准。

authors_text: Takyoung Kim,Hoonsang Yoon,Yukyung Lee,Pilsung Kang,Misuk Kim
title: Mismatch between Multi-turn Dialogue and its Evaluation Metric in Dialogue State Tracking
title_zh: 对话状态跟踪中多轮对话及其评估指标的不匹配
abstract: Dialogue state tracking (DST) aims to extract essential information from multi-turn dialogue situations and take appropriate actions. A belief state, one of the core pieces of information, refers to the subject and its specific content, and appears in the form of domain-slot-value. The trained model predicts "accumulated" belief states in every turn, and joint goal accuracy and slot accuracy are mainly used to evaluate the prediction; however, we specify that the current evaluation metrics have a critical limitation when evaluating belief states accumulated as the dialogue proceeds, especially in the most used MultiWOZ dataset. Additionally, we propose relative slot accuracy to complement existing metrics. Relative slot accuracy does not depend on the number of predefined slots, and allows intuitive evaluation by assigning relative scores according to the turn of each dialogue. This study also encourages not solely the reporting of joint goal accuracy, but also various complementary metrics in DST tasks for the sake of a realistic evaluation.
summary: Dialogue state tracking (DST) aims to extract essential information from multi-turn dialogue situations and take appropriate actions. A belief state, one of the core pieces of information, refers to the subject and its specific content, and appears in the form of domain-slot-value. The trained model predicts "accumulated" belief states in every turn, and joint goal accuracy and slot accuracy are mainly used to evaluate the prediction; however, we specify that the current evaluation metrics have a critical limitation when evaluating belief states accumulated as the dialogue proceeds, especially in the most used MultiWOZ dataset. Additionally, we propose relative slot accuracy to complement existing metrics. Relative slot accuracy does not depend on the number of predefined slots, and allows intuitive evaluation by assigning relative scores according to the turn of each dialogue. This study also encourages not solely the reporting of joint goal accuracy, but also various complementary metrics in DST tasks for the sake of a realistic evaluation.
abstract_zh: 对话状态跟踪（DST）旨在从多轮对话情况中提取必要信息并采取适当的行动。信念状态是核心信息之一，指的是主题及其具体内容，以域-槽-值的形式出现。训练好的模型每轮都会预测“累积”的信念状态，主要用联合目标准确率和槽位准确率来评估预测；然而，我们指出，当前的评估指标在评估对话过程中积累的信念状态时有一个关键的限制，特别是在最常用的 MultiWOZ 数据集中。此外，我们提出相对时隙精度来补充现有指标。相对槽准确度不依赖于预定义槽的数量，并且可以通过根据每个对话的轮次分配相对分数来进行直观评估。这项研究不仅鼓励报告联合目标准确性，还鼓励报告 DST 任务中的各种补充指标，以便进行现实评估。

AI.tldr: 本文探讨了对话状态跟踪任务中多轮对话与评估指标之间的不匹配问题，并提出新的评估方法。
AI.motivation: 现有评估指标（如联合目标准确率）在衡量多轮对话累积信念状态时存在关键局限，尤其在常用数据集MultiWOZ中表现不足。
AI.method: 提出相对槽位准确率指标，该指标不依赖预定义槽位数量，通过按对话轮次分配相对分数进行直观评估。
AI.result: 新指标有效补充了传统评估方法，能够更真实反映模型在多轮对话中的表现。
AI.conclusion: 应避免仅依赖联合目标准确率，需在对话状态跟踪任务中结合多种互补性指标进行综合评估。

authors_text: Shengshan Hu,Xiaogeng Liu,Yechao Zhang,Minghui Li,Leo Yu Zhang,Hai Jin,Libing Wu
title: Protecting Facial Privacy: Generating Adversarial Identity Masks via Style-robust Makeup Transfer
title_zh: 保护面部隐私：通过风格稳健的化妆转移生成对抗性身份面具
comment: Accepted by CVPR2022. Code is available at https://github.com/CGCL-codes/AMT-GAN
comments: Accepted by CVPR2022. Code is available at https://github.com/CGCL-codes/AMT-GAN
abstract: While deep face recognition (FR) systems have shown amazing performance in identification and verification, they also arouse privacy concerns for their excessive surveillance on users, especially for public face images widely spread on social networks. Recently, some studies adopt adversarial examples to protect photos from being identified by unauthorized face recognition systems. However, existing methods of generating adversarial face images suffer from many limitations, such as awkward visual, white-box setting, weak transferability, making them difficult to be applied to protect face privacy in reality. In this paper, we propose adversarial makeup transfer GAN (AMT-GAN), a novel face protection method aiming at constructing adversarial face images that preserve stronger black-box transferability and better visual quality simultaneously. AMT-GAN leverages generative adversarial networks (GAN) to synthesize adversarial face images with makeup transferred from reference images. In particular, we introduce a new regularization module along with a joint training strategy to reconcile the conflicts between the adversarial noises and the cycle consistence loss in makeup transfer, achieving a desirable balance between the attack strength and visual changes. Extensive experiments verify that compared with state of the arts, AMT-GAN can not only preserve a comfortable visual quality, but also achieve a higher attack success rate over commercial FR APIs, including Face++, Aliyun, and Microsoft.
summary: While deep face recognition (FR) systems have shown amazing performance in identification and verification, they also arouse privacy concerns for their excessive surveillance on users, especially for public face images widely spread on social networks. Recently, some studies adopt adversarial examples to protect photos from being identified by unauthorized face recognition systems. However, existing methods of generating adversarial face images suffer from many limitations, such as awkward visual, white-box setting, weak transferability, making them difficult to be applied to protect face privacy in reality. In this paper, we propose adversarial makeup transfer GAN (AMT-GAN), a novel face protection method aiming at constructing adversarial face images that preserve stronger black-box transferability and better visual quality simultaneously. AMT-GAN leverages generative adversarial networks (GAN) to synthesize adversarial face images with makeup transferred from reference images. In particular, we introduce a new regularization module along with a joint training strategy to reconcile the conflicts between the adversarial noises and the cycle consistence loss in makeup transfer, achieving a desirable balance between the attack strength and visual changes. Extensive experiments verify that compared with state of the arts, AMT-GAN can not only preserve a comfortable visual quality, but also achieve a higher attack success rate over commercial FR APIs, including Face++, Aliyun, and Microsoft.
abstract_zh: 虽然深度人脸识别（FR）系统在识别和验证方面表现出了惊人的性能，但它们也因其对用户的过度监视而引起隐私担忧，特别是对于社交网络上广泛传播的公开人脸图像。最近，一些研究采用对抗性例子来保护照片不被未经授权的人脸识别系统识别。然而，现有的生成对抗性人脸图像的方法存在许多局限性，例如视觉效果不佳、白盒设置、可迁移性弱等，使其难以应用于现实中的人脸隐私保护。在本文中，我们提出了对抗性化妆迁移 GAN（AMT-GAN），这是一种新颖的面部保护方法，旨在构建同时保留更强的黑盒可迁移性和更好的视觉质量的对抗性面部图像。 AMT-GAN 利用生成对抗网络 (GAN) 来合成对抗性人脸图像，并从参考图像转移化妆。特别是，我们引入了一个新的正则化模块和联合训练策略，以协调化妆转移中对抗性噪声和循环一致性损失之间的冲突，从而在攻击强度和视觉变化之间实现理想的平衡。大量实验验证，与现有技术相比，AMT-GAN不仅可以保持舒适的视觉质量，而且比商业FR API（包括Face++、阿里云和微软）获得更高的攻击成功率。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种通过风格鲁棒化妆迁移生成对抗性身份面具的方法，以保护面部隐私。
AI.motivation: 深度人脸识别系统引发隐私担忧，现有对抗样本方法存在视觉不自然、白盒设置限制及迁移性弱等问题，难以实际应用。
AI.method: 设计对抗化妆迁移生成对抗网络（AMT-GAN），结合正则化模块与联合训练策略，平衡对抗噪声与化妆迁移的循环一致性损失。
AI.result: 实验表明，AMT-GAN在保持视觉质量的同时，对Face++、Aliyun等商业人脸识别API的攻击成功率显著高于现有方法。
AI.conclusion: AMT-GAN通过风格鲁棒的化妆迁移有效解决了对抗样本的视觉质量与迁移性矛盾，为面部隐私保护提供了新方案。

search_text: Protecting Facial Privacy: Generating Adversarial Identity Masks via Style-robust Makeup Transfer 保护面部隐私：通过风格稳健的化妆转移生成对抗性身份面具 While deep face recognition (FR) systems have shown amazing performance in identification and verification, they also arouse privacy concerns for their excessive surveillance on users, especially for public face images widely spread on social networks. Recently, some studies adopt adversarial examples to protect photos from being identified by unauthorized face recognition systems. However, existing methods of generating adversarial face images suffer from many limitations, such as awkward visual, white-box setting, weak transferability, making them difficult to be applied to protect face privacy in reality. In this paper, we propose adversarial makeup transfer GAN (AMT-GAN), a novel face protection method aiming at constructing adversarial face images that preserve stronger black-box transferability and better visual quality simultaneously. AMT-GAN leverages generative adversarial networks (GAN) to synthesize adversarial face images with makeup transferred from reference images. In particular, we introduce a new regularization module along with a joint training strategy to reconcile the conflicts between the adversarial noises and the cycle consistence loss in makeup transfer, achieving a desirable balance between the attack strength and visual changes. Extensive experiments verify that compared with state of the arts, AMT-GAN can not only preserve a comfortable visual quality, but also achieve a higher attack success rate over commercial FR APIs, including Face++, Aliyun, and Microsoft. 虽然深度人脸识别（FR）系统在识别和验证方面表现出了惊人的性能，但它们也因其对用户的过度监视而引起隐私担忧，特别是对于社交网络上广泛传播的公开人脸图像。最近，一些研究采用对抗性例子来保护照片不被未经授权的人脸识别系统识别。然而，现有的生成对抗性人脸图像的方法存在许多局限性，例如视觉效果不佳、白盒设置、可迁移性弱等，使其难以应用于现实中的人脸隐私保护。在本文中，我们提出了对抗性化妆迁移 GAN（AMT-GAN），这是一种新颖的面部保护方法，旨在构建同时保留更强的黑盒可迁移性和更好的视觉质量的对抗性面部图像。 AMT-GAN 利用生成对抗网络 (GAN) 来合成对抗性人脸图像，并从参考图像转移化妆。特别是，我们引入了一个新的正则化模块和联合训练策略，以协调化妆转移中对抗性噪声和循环一致性损失之间的冲突，从而在攻击强度和视觉变化之间实现理想的平衡。大量实验验证，与现有技术相比，AMT-GAN不仅可以保持舒适的视觉质量，而且比商业FR API（包括Face++、阿里云和微软）获得更高的攻击成功率。 本文提出一种通过风格鲁棒化妆迁移生成对抗性身份面具的方法，以保护面部隐私。 Shengshan Hu,Xiaogeng Liu,Yechao Zhang,Minghui Li,Leo Yu Zhang,Hai Jin,Libing Wu cs.CV,cs.CR

authors_text: Anda Cheng,Peisong Wang,Xi Sheryl Zhang,Jian Cheng
title: Differentially Private Federated Learning with Local Regularization and Sparsification
title_zh: 具有局部正则化和稀疏化的差分私有联邦学习
abstract: User-level differential privacy (DP) provides certifiable privacy guarantees to the information that is specific to any user's data in federated learning. Existing methods that ensure user-level DP come at the cost of severe accuracy decrease. In this paper, we study the cause of model performance degradation in federated learning under user-level DP guarantee. We find the key to solving this issue is to naturally restrict the norm of local updates before executing operations that guarantee DP. To this end, we propose two techniques, Bounded Local Update Regularization and Local Update Sparsification, to increase model quality without sacrificing privacy. We provide theoretical analysis on the convergence of our framework and give rigorous privacy guarantees. Extensive experiments show that our framework significantly improves the privacy-utility trade-off over the state-of-the-arts for federated learning with user-level DP guarantee.
summary: User-level differential privacy (DP) provides certifiable privacy guarantees to the information that is specific to any user's data in federated learning. Existing methods that ensure user-level DP come at the cost of severe accuracy decrease. In this paper, we study the cause of model performance degradation in federated learning under user-level DP guarantee. We find the key to solving this issue is to naturally restrict the norm of local updates before executing operations that guarantee DP. To this end, we propose two techniques, Bounded Local Update Regularization and Local Update Sparsification, to increase model quality without sacrificing privacy. We provide theoretical analysis on the convergence of our framework and give rigorous privacy guarantees. Extensive experiments show that our framework significantly improves the privacy-utility trade-off over the state-of-the-arts for federated learning with user-level DP guarantee.
abstract_zh: 用户级差分隐私（DP）为联邦学习中任何用户数据特定的信息提供可证明的隐私保证。确保用户级 DP 的现有方法是以精度严重下降为代价的。本文研究了用户级DP保证下联邦学习中模型性能下降的原因。我们发现解决这个问题的关键是在执行保证DP的操作之前自然地限制本地更新的范数。为此，我们提出了两种技术：有界局部更新正则化和局部更新稀疏化，以在不牺牲隐私的情况下提高模型质量。我们对我们的框架的收敛性进行理论分析，并给出严格的隐私保证。大量实验表明，与具有用户级 DP 保证的联邦学习的最新技术相比，我们的框架显着改善了隐私与实用性的权衡。

AI.tldr: 本文旨在解决联邦学习中用户级差分隐私导致的模型性能下降问题，通过引入局部正则化和稀疏化技术提升模型效果。
AI.motivation: 现有用户级差分隐私方法在联邦学习中导致模型精度大幅下降，需寻找不牺牲隐私的情况下提升模型性能的方法。
AI.method: 提出局部更新正则化和稀疏化技术，在保证差分隐私的同时限制局部更新范数，提升模型质量。
AI.result: 实验表明，该框架在用户级差分隐私的联邦学习中显著提升了隐私与效用的平衡，优于现有最先进的方法。
AI.conclusion: 通过理论分析和实验验证，所提方法有效解决了用户级差分隐私下的联邦学习模型性能下降问题，实现了更优的隐私-效用权衡。

authors_text: Ran Wang,Kehan Du,Qianhe Chen,Yifei Zhao,Mojie Tang,Hongxi Tao,Shipan Wang,Yiyao Li,Yong Wang
title: RumorLens: Interactive Analysis and Validation of Suspected Rumors on Social Media
title_zh: RumorLens：社交媒体上疑似谣言的互动分析与验证
abstract: With the development of social media, various rumors can be easily spread on the Internet and such rumors can have serious negative effects on society. Thus, it has become a critical task for social media platforms to deal with suspected rumors. However, due to the lack of effective tools, it is often difficult for platform administrators to analyze and validate rumors from a large volume of information on a social media platform efficiently. We have worked closely with social media platform administrators for four months to summarize their requirements of identifying and analyzing rumors, and further proposed an interactive visual analytics system, RumorLens, to help them deal with the rumor efficiently and gain an in-depth understanding of the patterns of rumor spreading. RumorLens integrates natural language processing (NLP) and other data processing techniques with visualization techniques to facilitate interactive analysis and validation of suspected rumors. We propose well-coordinated visualizations to provide users with three levels of details of suspected rumors: an overview displays both spatial distribution and temporal evolution of suspected rumors; a projection view leverages a metaphor-based glyph to represent each suspected rumor and further enable users to gain a quick understanding of their overall characteristics and similarity with each other; a propagation view visualizes the dynamic spreading details of a suspected rumor with a novel circular visualization design, and facilitates interactive analysis and validation of rumors in a compact manner. By using a real-world dataset collected from Sina Weibo, one case study with a domain expert is conducted to evaluate

By using a real-world dataset collected from Sina Weibo, one case study with a domain expert is conducted to evaluate
abstract_zh: 随着社交媒体的发展，各种谣言很容易在网络上传播，这些谣言会给社会带来严重的负面影响。因此，应对疑似谣言已成为社交媒体平台的一项重要任务。然而，由于缺乏有效的工具，平台管理员往往很难从社交媒体平台上的大量信息中高效地分析和验证谣言。我们与社交媒体平台管理者紧密合作四个月，总结了他们对谣言识别和分析的需求，并进一步提出了交互式可视化分析系统RumorLens，帮助他们高效处理谣言，深入了解谣言传播的模式。 RumorLens将自然语言处理（NLP）和其他数据处理技术与可视化技术相结合，以方便对可疑谣言进行交互式分析和验证。我们提出了协调良好的可视化，为用户提供可疑谣言的三个层次的细节：概览显示可疑谣言的空间分布和时间演变；投影视图利用基于隐喻的字形来表示每个可疑的谣言，并进一步使用户能够快速了解​​它们的整体特征和彼此的相似性；传播视图以新颖的圆形可视化设计将疑似谣言的动态传播细节可视化，以紧凑的方式方便对谣言进行交互式分析和验证。通过使用从新浪微博收集的真实数据集，与领域专家进行了一个案例研究来评估

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种交互式可视化分析系统RumorLens，帮助社交媒体平台管理员高效分析和验证可疑谣言。
AI.motivation: 社交媒体上谣言传播广泛且危害大，但现有工具难以高效处理海量信息，管理员缺乏有效分析手段。
AI.method: 结合自然语言处理与可视化技术，设计包含空间时间概览、隐喻符号投影视图和动态传播视图的三级细节分析系统。
AI.result: 基于新浪微博真实数据集的案例研究表明，系统能有效辅助领域专家分析谣言传播模式并验证可疑信息。
AI.conclusion: RumorLens通过多层级可视化设计显著提升了谣言分析效率，为平台管理员提供了深入理解谣言传播规律的工具。

ai_content.tldr: 本文提出一种交互式可视化分析系统RumorLens，帮助社交媒体平台管理员高效分析和验证可疑谣言。
ai_content.motivation: 社交媒体上谣言传播广泛且危害大，但现有工具难以高效处理海量信息，管理员缺乏有效分析手段。

search_text: RumorLens: Interactive Analysis and Validation of Suspected Rumors on Social Media RumorLens：社交媒体上疑似谣言的互动分析与验证 With the development of social media, various rumors can be easily spread on the Internet and such rumors can have serious negative effects on society. Thus, it has become a critical task for social media platforms to deal with suspected rumors. However, due to the lack of effective tools, it is often difficult for platform administrators to analyze and validate rumors from a large volume of information on a social media platform efficiently. We have worked closely with social media platform administrators for four months to summarize their requirements of identifying and analyzing rumors, and further proposed an interactive visual analytics system, RumorLens, to help them deal with the rumor efficiently and gain an in-depth understanding of the patterns of rumor spreading. RumorLens integrates natural language processing (NLP) and other data processing techniques with visualization techniques to facilitate interactive analysis and validation of suspected rumors. We propose well-coordinated visualizations to provide users with three levels of details of suspected rumors: an overview displays both spatial distribution and temporal evolution of suspected rumors; a projection view leverages a metaphor-based glyph to represent each suspected rumor and further enable users to gain a quick understanding of their overall characteristics and similarity with each other; a propagation view visualizes the dynamic spreading details of a suspected rumor with a novel circular visualization design, and facilitates interactive analysis and validation of rumors in a compact manner. By using a real-world dataset collected from Sina Weibo, one case study with a domain expert is conducted to evaluate 随着社交媒体的发展，各种谣言很容易在网络上传播，这些谣言会给社会带来严重的负面影响。因此，应对疑似谣言已成为社交媒体平台的一项重要任务。然而，由于缺乏有效的工具，平台管理员往往很难从社交媒体平台上的大量信息中高效地分析和验证谣言。我们与社交媒体平台管理者紧密合作四个月，总结了他们对谣言识别和分析的需求，并进一步提出了交互式可视化分析系统RumorLens，帮助他们高效处理谣言，深入了解谣言传播的模式。 RumorLens将自然语言处理（NLP）和其他数据处理技术与可视化技术相结合，以方便对可疑谣言进行交互式分析和验证。我们提出了协调良好的可视化，为用户提供可疑谣言的三个层次的细节：概览显示可疑谣言的空间分布和时间演变；投影视图利用基于隐喻的字形来表示每个可疑的谣言，并进一步使用户能够快速了解​​它们的整体特征和彼此的相似性；传播视图以新颖的圆形可视化设计将疑似谣言的动态传播细节可视化，以紧凑的方式方便对谣言进行交互式分析和验证。通过使用从新浪微博收集的真实数据集，与领域专家进行了一个案例研究来评估 本文提出一种交互式可视化分析系统RumorLens，帮助社交媒体平台管理员高效分析和验证可疑谣言。 Ran Wang,Kehan Du,Qianhe Chen,Yifei Zhao,Mojie Tang,Hongxi Tao,Shipan Wang,Yiyao Li,Yong Wang cs.HC

authors_text: Lizong Zhang,Yiming Wang,Bei Hui,Xiujian Zhang,Sijuan Liu,Shuxin Feng
title: Behavior Recognition Based on the Integration of Multigranular Motion Features
title_zh: 基于多颗粒运动特征融合的行为识别
abstract: The recognition of behaviors in videos usually requires a combinatorial analysis of the spatial information about objects and their dynamic action information in the temporal dimension. Specifically, behavior recognition may even rely more on the modeling of temporal information containing short-range and long-range motions; this contrasts with computer vision tasks involving images that focus on the understanding of spatial information. However, current solutions fail to jointly and comprehensively analyze short-range motion between adjacent frames and long-range temporal aggregations at large scales in videos. In this paper, we propose a novel behavior recognition method based on the integration of multigranular (IMG) motion features. In particular, we achieve reliable motion information modeling through the synergy of a channel attention-based short-term motion feature enhancement module (CMEM) and a cascaded long-term motion feature integration module (CLIM). We evaluate our model on several action recognition benchmarks such as HMDB51, Something-Something and UCF101. The experimental results demonstrate that our approach outperforms the previous state-of-the-art methods, which confirms its effectiveness and efficiency.
summary: The recognition of behaviors in videos usually requires a combinatorial analysis of the spatial information about objects and their dynamic action information in the temporal dimension. Specifically, behavior recognition may even rely more on the modeling of temporal information containing short-range and long-range motions; this contrasts with computer vision tasks involving images that focus on the understanding of spatial information. However, current solutions fail to jointly and comprehensively analyze short-range motion between adjacent frames and long-range temporal aggregations at large scales in videos. In this paper, we propose a novel behavior recognition method based on the integration of multigranular (IMG) motion features. In particular, we achieve reliable motion information modeling through the synergy of a channel attention-based short-term motion feature enhancement module (CMEM) and a cascaded long-term motion feature integration module (CLIM). We evaluate our model on several action recognition benchmarks such as HMDB51, Something-Something and UCF101. The experimental results demonstrate that our approach outperforms the previous state-of-the-art methods, which confirms its effectiveness and efficiency.
abstract_zh: 视频中行为的识别通常需要对对象的空间信息及其在时间维度上的动态动作信息进行组合分析。具体来说，行为识别甚至可能更多地依赖于包含短程和长程运动的时间信息的建模；这与涉及专注于理解空间信息的图像的计算机视觉任务形成鲜明对比。然而，当前的解决方案无法联合、全面地分析视频中相邻帧之间的短程运动和大范围的长程时间聚合。在本文中，我们提出了一种基于多粒度（IMG）运动特征集成的新型行为识别方法。特别是，我们通过基于通道注意的短期运动特征增强模块（CMEM）和级联长期运动特征集成模块（CLIM）的协同作用实现了可靠的运动信息建模。我们在多个动作识别基准（例如 HMDB51、Something-Something 和 UCF101）上评估我们的模型。实验结果表明，我们的方法优于以前最先进的方法，这证实了其有效性和效率。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于多粒度运动特征融合的行为识别方法，通过结合短期和长期运动特征提升视频行为识别效果。
AI.motivation: 现有方法未能有效联合分析视频中相邻帧的短期运动与大尺度的长期时序信息，导致行为识别性能受限。
AI.method: 设计通道注意力机制的短期运动特征增强模块（CMEM）与级联长期运动特征融合模块（CLIM），实现多粒度运动特征的协同建模。
AI.result: 在HMDB51、Something-Something和UCF101数据集上均优于现有最先进方法，验证了方法的有效性与高效性。
AI.conclusion: 通过整合短期与长期运动特征，所提方法显著提升了视频行为识别的准确率与鲁棒性。

authors_text: Hao-Ya Hsueh,Alexandru-Iosif Toma,Hussein Ali Jaafar,Edward Stow,Riku Murai,Paul H. J. Kelly,Sajad Saeedi
title: Systematic Comparison of Path Planning Algorithms using PathBench
title_zh: 使用 PathBench 系统比较路径规划算法
comment: Accepted to Advanced Robotics Journal; 23 pages, 9 figures, 4 tables. arXiv admin note: substantial text overlap with arXiv:2105.01777
comments: Accepted to Advanced Robotics Journal; 23 pages, 9 figures, 4 tables. arXiv admin note: substantial text overlap with arXiv:2105.01777
abstract: Path planning is an essential component of mobile robotics. Classical path planning algorithms, such as wavefront and rapidly-exploring random tree (RRT) are used heavily in autonomous robots. With the recent advances in machine learning, development of learning-based path planning algorithms has been experiencing rapid growth. An unified path planning interface that facilitates the development and benchmarking of existing and new algorithms is needed. This paper presents PathBench, a platform for developing, visualizing, training, testing, and benchmarking of existing and future, classical and learning-based path planning algorithms in 2D and 3D grid world environments. Many existing path planning algorithms are supported; e.g. A*, Dijkstra, waypoint planning networks, value iteration networks, gated path planning networks; and integrating new algorithms is easy and clearly specified. The benchmarking ability of PathBench is explored in this paper by comparing algorithms across five different hardware systems and three different map types, including built-in PathBench maps, video game maps, and maps from real world databases. Metrics, such as path length, success rate, and computational time, were used to evaluate algorithms. Algorithmic analysis was also performed on a real world robot to demonstrate PathBench's support for Robot Operating System (ROS). PathBench is open source.
summary: Path planning is an essential component of mobile robotics. Classical path planning algorithms, such as wavefront and rapidly-exploring random tree (RRT) are used heavily in autonomous robots. With the recent advances in machine learning, development of learning-based path planning algorithms has been experiencing rapid growth. An unified path planning interface that facilitates the development and benchmarking of existing and new algorithms is needed. This paper presents PathBench, a platform for developing, visualizing, training, testing, and benchmarking of existing and future, classical and learning-based path planning algorithms in 2D and 3D grid world environments. Many existing path planning algorithms are supported; e.g. A*, Dijkstra, waypoint planning networks, value iteration networks, gated path planning networks; and integrating new algorithms is easy and clearly specified. The benchmarking ability of PathBench is explored in this paper by comparing algorithms across five different hardware systems and three different map types, including built-in PathBench maps, video game maps, and maps from real world databases. Metrics, such as path length, success rate, and computational time, were used to evaluate algorithms. Algorithmic analysis was also performed on a real world robot to demonstrate PathBench's support for Robot Operating System (ROS). PathBench is open source.
abstract_zh: 路径规划是移动机器人技术的重要组成部分。经典路径规划算法，例如波前和快速探索随机树（RRT），在自主机器人中大量使用。随着机器学习的最新进展，基于学习的路径规划算法的发展一直在快速增长。需要一个统一的路径规划界面，以促进现有和新算法的开发和基准测试。本文介绍了 PathBench，这是一个用于在 2D 和 3D 网格世界环境中开发、可视化、训练、测试和基准测试现有和未来、经典和基于学习的路径规划算法的平台。支持多种现有的路径规划算法；例如A*、Dijkstra、航路点规划网络、价值迭代网络、门控路径规划网络；集成新算法非常简单且明确。本文通过比较五种不同硬件系统和三种不同地图类型（包括内置 PathBench 地图、视频游戏地图和来自现实世界数据库的地图）的算法，探讨了 PathBench 的基准测试能力。路径长度、成功率和计算时间等指标用于评估算法。还对现实世界的机器人进行了算法分析，以证明 PathBench 对机器人操作系统 (ROS) 的支持。 PathBench 是开源的。

AI.tldr: 本文提出PathBench平台，用于系统比较二维和三维网格环境中经典与基于学习的路径规划算法的性能。
AI.motivation: 经典路径规划算法与新兴机器学习方法缺乏统一的开发和基准测试平台，需支持多硬件系统和地图类型的算法比较。
AI.method: 开发PathBench平台，集成多种算法（如A*、RRT、价值迭代网络等），通过五种硬件系统和三种地图类型（内置地图、游戏地图、真实地图）进行基准测试，使用路径长度、成功率和计算时间等指标评估。
AI.result: 实验结果展示了不同算法在不同环境下的性能差异，验证了PathBench在ROS机器人上的实用性，并证明其支持算法快速集成与对比分析的能力。
AI.conclusion: PathBench为路径规划算法研究提供了开源、统一的基准测试平台，有助于推动经典与基于学习方法的性能优化与应用探索。

search_text: Systematic Comparison of Path Planning Algorithms using PathBench 使用 PathBench 系统比较路径规划算法 Path planning is an essential component of mobile robotics. Classical path planning algorithms, such as wavefront and rapidly-exploring random tree (RRT) are used heavily in autonomous robots. With the recent advances in machine learning, development of learning-based path planning algorithms has been experiencing rapid growth. An unified path planning interface that facilitates the development and benchmarking of existing and new algorithms is needed. This paper presents PathBench, a platform for developing, visualizing, training, testing, and benchmarking of existing and future, classical and learning-based path planning algorithms in 2D and 3D grid world environments. Many existing path planning algorithms are supported; e.g. A*, Dijkstra, waypoint planning networks, value iteration networks, gated path planning networks; and integrating new algorithms is easy and clearly specified. The benchmarking ability of PathBench is explored in this paper by comparing algorithms across five different hardware systems and three different map types, including built-in PathBench maps, video game maps, and maps from real world databases. Metrics, such as path length, success rate, and computational time, were used to evaluate algorithms. Algorithmic analysis was also performed on a real world robot to demonstrate PathBench's support for Robot Operating System (ROS). PathBench is open source. 路径规划是移动机器人技术的重要组成部分。经典路径规划算法，例如波前和快速探索随机树（RRT），在自主机器人中大量使用。随着机器学习的最新进展，基于学习的路径规划算法的发展一直在快速增长。需要一个统一的路径规划界面，以促进现有和新算法的开发和基准测试。本文介绍了 PathBench，这是一个用于在 2D 和 3D 网格世界环境中开发、可视化、训练、测试和基准测试现有和未来、经典和基于学习的路径规划算法的平台。支持多种现有的路径规划算法；例如A*、Dijkstra、航路点规划网络、价值迭代网络、门控路径规划网络；集成新算法非常简单且明确。本文通过比较五种不同硬件系统和三种不同地图类型（包括内置 PathBench 地图、视频游戏地图和来自现实世界数据库的地图）的算法，探讨了 PathBench 的基准测试能力。路径长度、成功率和计算时间等指标用于评估算法。还对现实世界的机器人进行了算法分析，以证明 PathBench 对机器人操作系统 (ROS) 的支持。 PathBench 是开源的。 本文提出PathBench平台，用于系统比较二维和三维网格环境中经典与基于学习的路径规划算法的性能。 Hao-Ya Hsueh,Alexandru-Iosif Toma,Hussein Ali Jaafar,Edward Stow,Riku Murai,Paul H. J. Kelly,Sajad Saeedi cs.RO,cs.AI

authors_text: Yang You,Ruoxi Shi,Weiming Wang,Cewu Lu
title: CPPF: Towards Robust Category-Level 9D Pose Estimation in the Wild
title_zh: CPPF：在野外实现稳健的类别级 9D 姿势估计
abstract: In this paper, we tackle the problem of category-level 9D pose estimation in the wild, given a single RGB-D frame. Using supervised data of real-world 9D poses is tedious and erroneous, and also fails to generalize to unseen scenarios. Besides, category-level pose estimation requires a method to be able to generalize to unseen objects at test time, which is also challenging. Drawing inspirations from traditional point pair features (PPFs), in this paper, we design a novel Category-level PPF (CPPF) voting method to achieve accurate, robust and generalizable 9D pose estimation in the wild. To obtain robust pose estimation, we sample numerous point pairs on an object, and for each pair our model predicts necessary SE(3)-invariant voting statistics on object centers, orientations and scales. A novel coarse-to-fine voting algorithm is proposed to eliminate noisy point pair samples and generate final predictions from the population. To get rid of false positives in the orientation voting process, an auxiliary binary disambiguating classification task is introduced for each sampled point pair. In order to detect objects in the wild, we carefully design our sim-to-real pipeline by training on synthetic point clouds only, unless objects have ambiguous poses in geometry. Under this circumstance, color information is leveraged to disambiguate these poses. Results on standard benchmarks show that our method is on par with current state of the arts with real-world training data. Extensive experiments further show that our method is robust to noise and gives promising results under extremely challenging scenarios. Our code is available on https://github.com/qq456cvb/CPPF.
summary: In this paper, we tackle the problem of category-level 9D pose estimation in the wild, given a single RGB-D frame. Using supervised data of real-world 9D poses is tedious and erroneous, and also fails to generalize to unseen scenarios. Besides, category-level pose estimation requires a method to be able to generalize to unseen objects at test time, which is also challenging. Drawing inspirations from traditional point pair features (PPFs), in this paper, we design a novel Category-level PPF (CPPF) voting method to achieve accurate, robust and generalizable 9D pose estimation in the wild. To obtain robust pose estimation, we sample numerous point pairs on an object, and for each pair our model predicts necessary SE(3)-invariant voting statistics on object centers, orientations and scales. A novel coarse-to-fine voting algorithm is proposed to eliminate noisy point pair samples and generate final predictions from the population. To get rid of false positives in the orientation voting process, an auxiliary binary disambiguating classification task is introduced for each sampled point pair. In order to detect objects in the wild, we carefully design our sim-to-real pipeline by training on synthetic point clouds only, unless objects have ambiguous poses in geometry. Under this circumstance, color information is leveraged to disambiguate these poses. Results on standard benchmarks show that our method is on par with current state of the arts with real-world training data. Extensive experiments further show that our method is robust to noise and gives promising results under extremely challenging scenarios. Our code is available on https://github.com/qq456cvb/CPPF.
abstract_zh: 在本文中，我们解决了给定单个 RGB-D 帧的情况下类别级 9D 姿态估计的问题。使用真实世界 9D 姿势的监督数据是乏味且错误的，而且也无法推广到未见过的场景。此外，类别级姿态估计需要一种能够泛化到测试时未见过的物体的方法，这也具有挑战性。受到传统点对特征（PPF）的启发，在本文中，我们设计了一种新颖的类别级 PPF（CPPF）投票方法，以在野外实现准确、稳健和可推广的 9D 姿态估计。为了获得稳健的姿态估计，我们对物体上的大量点对进行采样，并且对于每一对，我们的模型预测关于物体中心、方向和尺度的必要的 SE(3) 不变投票统计数据。提出了一种新颖的从粗到细的投票算法来消除噪声点对样本并从总体中生成最终预测。为了消除方向投票过程中的误报，为每个采样点对引入了辅助二元消歧分类任务。为了检测野外的物体，我们通过仅在合成点云上进行训练来仔细设计我们的模拟到真实的管道，除非物体在几何中具有不明确的姿势。在这种情况下，利用颜色信息来消除这些姿势的歧义。标准基准的结果表明，我们的方法与现实世界训练数据的当前技术水平相当。大量的实验进一步表明，我们的方法对噪声具有鲁棒性，并且在极具挑战性的情况下给出了有希望的结果。我们的代码可以在 https://github.com/qq456cvb/CPPF 上找到。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种名为CPPF的新方法，用于在真实场景中实现鲁棒的类别级9D姿态估计。
AI.motivation: 传统方法依赖真实世界监督数据进行9D姿态估计，但存在泛化能力差、标注困难等问题，且类别级估计需应对测试时的未知物体挑战。
AI.method: 设计基于点对特征（PPF）的投票方法CPPF，通过采样点对预测SE(3)-不变统计量，结合粗到细投票算法消除噪声，并引入二分类任务过滤伪阳性，利用合成数据与颜色信息构建Sim-to-Real训练管道。
AI.result: 在标准基准测试中性能与使用真实数据的最先进方法相当，且在噪声和极端复杂场景下表现稳健。
AI.conclusion: CPPF通过合成数据训练实现了鲁棒的类别级姿态估计，为真实场景应用提供了有效解决方案。

authors_text: Abduallah Mohamed,Fernando Lejarza,Stephanie Cahail,Christian Claudel,Edison Thomaz
title: HAR-GCNN: Deep Graph CNNs for Human Activity Recognition From Highly Unlabeled Mobile Sensor Data
title_zh: HAR-GCNN：用于根据高度未标记的移动传感器数据进行人类活动识别的深度图 CNN
comment: ef:2022 IEEE International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Communications Workshops and other Affiliated Events (PerCom Workshops)
comments: ef:2022 IEEE International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Communications Workshops and other Affiliated Events (PerCom Workshops)
abstract: The problem of human activity recognition from mobile sensor data applies to multiple domains, such as health monitoring, personal fitness, daily life logging, and senior care. A critical challenge for training human activity recognition models is data quality. Acquiring balanced datasets containing accurate activity labels requires humans to correctly annotate and potentially interfere with the subjects' normal activities in real-time. Despite the likelihood of incorrect annotation or lack thereof, there is often an inherent chronology to human behavior. For example, we take a shower after we exercise. This implicit chronology can be used to learn unknown labels and classify future activities. In this work, we propose HAR-GCCN, a deep graph CNN model that leverages the correlation between chronologically adjacent sensor measurements to predict the correct labels for unclassified activities that have at least one activity label. We propose a new training strategy enforcing that the model predicts the missing activity labels by leveraging the known ones. HAR-GCCN shows superior performance relative to previously used baseline methods, improving classification accuracy by about 25% and up to 68% on different datasets. Code is available at \url{https://github.com/abduallahmohamed/HAR-GCNN}.
summary: The problem of human activity recognition from mobile sensor data applies to multiple domains, such as health monitoring, personal fitness, daily life logging, and senior care. A critical challenge for training human activity recognition models is data quality. Acquiring balanced datasets containing accurate activity labels requires humans to correctly annotate and potentially interfere with the subjects' normal activities in real-time. Despite the likelihood of incorrect annotation or lack thereof, there is often an inherent chronology to human behavior. For example, we take a shower after we exercise. This implicit chronology can be used to learn unknown labels and classify future activities. In this work, we propose HAR-GCCN, a deep graph CNN model that leverages the correlation between chronologically adjacent sensor measurements to predict the correct labels for unclassified activities that have at least one activity label. We propose a new training strategy enforcing that the model predicts the missing activity labels by leveraging the known ones. HAR-GCCN shows superior performance relative to previously used baseline methods, improving classification accuracy by about 25% and up to 68% on different datasets. Code is available at \url{https://github.com/abduallahmohamed/HAR-GCNN}.
abstract_zh: 从移动传感器数据中识别人类活动的问题适用于多个领域，例如健康监测、个人健身、日常生活记录和老年护理。训练人类活动识别模型的一个关键挑战是数据质量。获取包含准确活动标签的平衡数据集需要人类正确注释并可能实时干扰受试者的正常活动。尽管注释可能不正确或缺乏注释，但人类行为通常存在固有的时间顺序。例如，我们运动后会洗澡。这种隐式年表可用于学习未知标签并对未来活动进行分类。在这项工作中，我们提出了 HAR-GCCN，这是一种深度图 CNN 模型，它利用按时间顺序相邻的传感器测量之间的相关性来预测具有至少一个活动标签的未分类活动的正确标签。我们提出了一种新的训练策略，强制模型通过利用已知的活动标签来预测缺失的活动标签。相对于之前使用的基线方法，HAR-GCCN 表现出卓越的性能，在不同数据集上的分类精度提高了约 25%，最高可达 68%。代码可在 \url{https://github.com/abduallahmohamed/HAR-GCNN} 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出HAR-GCCN模型，利用移动传感器数据中的时间相关性进行人类活动识别，特别针对高度未标记的数据集。
AI.motivation: 传统活动识别依赖精确标注的数据，但实际中标签获取困难且可能存在错误。人类行为具有内在时间顺序（如运动后洗澡），可利用此特性提升模型性能。
AI.method: 设计基于图卷积神经网络（GCNN）的HAR-GCCN模型，通过时间相邻传感器数据的关联性预测未标记活动标签，并采用新训练策略强化已知标签对缺失标签的约束。
AI.result: 在多个数据集上分类准确率提升25%-68%，优于现有基线方法，代码已开源。
AI.conclusion: HAR-GCCN通过挖掘时间序列数据的隐含规律，有效解决了活动识别中未标记数据的标注难题，为弱监督学习提供了新思路。

Despite the likelihood of incorrect annotation or lack thereof, there is often an inherent chronology to human behavior. For example, we take a shower after we exercise. This implicit chronology can be used to learn unknown labels and classify future activities. In this work, we propose HAR-GCCN, a deep graph CNN model that leverages the correlation between chronologically adjacent sensor measurements to predict the correct labels for unclassified activities that have at least one activity label. We propose a new training strategy enforcing that the model predicts the missing activity labels by leveraging the known ones. HAR-GCCN shows superior performance relative to previously used baseline methods, improving classification accuracy by about 25% and up to 68% on different datasets. Code is available at \url{https://github.com/abduallahmohamed/HAR-GCNN}. 从移动传感器数据中识别人类活动的问题适用于多个领域，例如健康监测、个人健身、日常生活记录和老年护理。训练人类活动识别模型的一个关键挑战是数据质量。获取包含准确活动标签的平衡数据集需要人类正确注释并可能实时干扰受试者的正常活动。尽管注释可能不正确或缺乏注释，但人类行为通常存在固有的时间顺序。例如，我们运动后会洗澡。这种隐式年表可用于学习未知标签并对未来活动进行分类。在这项工作中，我们提出了 HAR-GCCN，这是一种深度图 CNN 模型，它利用按时间顺序相邻的传感器测量之间的相关性来预测具有至少一个活动标签的未分类活动的正确标签。我们提出了一种新的训练策略，强制模型通过利用已知的活动标签来预测缺失的活动标签。相对于之前使用的基线方法，HAR-GCCN 表现出卓越的性能，在不同数据集上的分类精度提高了约 25%，最高可达 68%。代码可在 \url{https://github.com/abduallahmohamed/HAR-GCNN} 获取。 本文提出HAR-GCCN模型，利用移动传感器数据中的时间相关性进行人类活动识别，特别针对高度未标记的数据集。 Abduallah Mohamed,Fernando Lejarza,Stephanie Cahail,Christian Claudel,Edison Thomaz cs.CV,cs.AI,cs.HC,cs.LG

authors_text: Kareem Metwaly,Aerin Kim,Elliot Branson,Vishal Monga
title: GlideNet: Global, Local and Intrinsic based Dense Embedding NETwork for Multi-category Attributes Prediction
title_zh: GlideNet：用于多类别属性预测的基于全局、局部和内在的密集嵌入网络
comment: CVPR 2022, 16 pages (including supplementary), CAR Dataset, VAW Dataset, http://signal.ee.psu.edu/research/glidenet.html
comments: CVPR 2022, 16 pages (including supplementary), CAR Dataset, VAW Dataset, http://signal.ee.psu.edu/research/glidenet.html
abstract: Attaching attributes (such as color, shape, state, action) to object categories is an important computer vision problem. Attribute prediction has seen exciting recent progress and is often formulated as a multi-label classification problem. Yet significant challenges remain in: 1) predicting diverse attributes over multiple categories, 2) modeling attributes-category dependency, 3) capturing both global and local scene context, and 4) predicting attributes of objects with low pixel-count. To address these issues, we propose a novel multi-category attribute prediction deep architecture named GlideNet, which contains three distinct feature extractors. A global feature extractor recognizes what objects are present in a scene, whereas a local one focuses on the area surrounding the object of interest. Meanwhile, an intrinsic feature extractor uses an extension of standard convolution dubbed Informed Convolution to retrieve features of objects with low pixel-count. GlideNet uses gating mechanisms with binary masks and its self-learned category embedding to combine the dense embeddings. Collectively, the Global-Local-Intrinsic blocks comprehend the scene's global context while attending to the characteristics of the local object of interest. Finally, using the combined features, an interpreter predicts the attributes, and the length of the output is determined by the category, thereby removing unnecessary attributes. GlideNet can achieve compelling results on two recent and challenging datasets -- VAW and CAR -- for large-scale attribute prediction. For instance, it obtains more than 5\% gain over state of the art in the mean recall (mR) metric. GlideNet's advantages are especially apparent when predicting attributes of objects with low pixel counts as well as attributes that demand global context understanding. Finally, we show that GlideNet excels in training starved real-world scenarios.

Attribute prediction has seen exciting recent progress and is often formulated as a multi-label classification problem. Yet significant challenges remain in: 1) predicting diverse attributes over multiple categories, 2) modeling attributes-category dependency, 3) capturing both global and local scene context, and 4) predicting attributes of objects with low pixel-count. To address these issues, we propose a novel multi-category attribute prediction deep architecture named GlideNet, which contains three distinct feature extractors. A global feature extractor recognizes what objects are present in a scene, whereas a local one focuses on the area surrounding the object of interest. Meanwhile, an intrinsic feature extractor uses an extension of standard convolution dubbed Informed Convolution to retrieve features of objects with low pixel-count. GlideNet uses gating mechanisms with binary masks and its self-learned category embedding to combine the dense embeddings. Collectively, the Global-Local-Intrinsic blocks comprehend the scene's global context while attending to the characteristics of the local object of interest. Finally, using the combined features, an interpreter predicts the attributes, and the length of the output is determined by the category, thereby removing unnecessary attributes. GlideNet can achieve compelling results on two recent and challenging datasets -- VAW and CAR -- for large-scale attribute prediction. For instance, it obtains more than 5\% gain over state of the art in the mean recall (mR) metric. GlideNet's advantages are especially apparent when predicting attributes of objects with low pixel counts as well as attributes that demand global context understanding. Finally, we show that GlideNet excels in training starved real-world scenarios.
abstract_zh: 将属性（例如颜色、形状、状态、动作）附加到对象类别是一个重要的计算机视觉问题。属性预测最近取得了令人兴奋的进展，并且通常被表述为多标签分类问题。然而，重大挑战仍然存在：1）预测多个类别的不同属性，2）建模属性类别依赖性，3）捕获全局和局部场景上下文，以及4）预测低像素数对象的属性。为了解决这些问题，我们提出了一种名为 GlideNet 的新型多类别属性预测深度架构，其中包含三个不同的特征提取器。全局特征提取器识别场景中存在哪些对象，而局部特征提取器则关注感兴趣对象周围的区域。同时，内在特征提取器使用称为“知情卷积”的标准卷积扩展来检索低像素数对象的特征。 GlideNet 使用带有二进制掩码的门控机制及其自学习的类别嵌入来组合密集嵌入。总的来说，全局-局部-内在块理解场景的全局上下文，同时关注局部感兴趣对象的特征。最后，使用组合的特征，解释器预测属性，并且输出的长度由类别确定，从而去除不必要的属性。 GlideNet 可以在两个最近且具有挑战性的数据集（VAW 和 CAR）上取得引人注目的结果，用于大规模属性预测。例如，它在平均召回率 (mR) 指标方面比现有技术提高了 5% 以上。在预测低像素数对象的属性以及需要全局上下文理解的属性时，GlideNet 的优势尤其明显。最后，我们展示了 GlideNet 在训练匮乏的现实场景方面表现出色。

AI.tldr: 提出一种名为GlideNet的深度网络架构，用于多类别属性预测，解决跨类别属性预测、属性-类别依赖建模、全局与局部上下文捕捉及低像素物体属性预测的挑战。
AI.motivation: 现有方法在跨类别属性预测、属性-类别依赖建模、全局局部上下文捕捉及低像素物体属性预测方面存在挑战，需更高效模型。
AI.method: 设计包含全局、局部和内在特征提取器的GlideNet，结合门控机制与自学习类别嵌入，整合多尺度特征以预测属性。
AI.result: 在VAW和CAR数据集上平均召回率超越现有方法5%，尤其在低像素物体及需全局理解的属性预测中表现突出。
AI.conclusion: GlideNet在大规模属性预测及数据稀缺场景下效果显著，具有实际应用价值。

authors_text: Alexander Long,Alan Blair,Herke van Hoof
title: Fast and Data Efficient Reinforcement Learning from Pixels via Non-Parametric Value Approximation
title_zh: 通过非参数值近似从像素进行快速且数据高效的强化学习
abstract: We present Nonparametric Approximation of Inter-Trace returns (NAIT), a Reinforcement Learning algorithm for discrete action, pixel-based environments that is both highly sample and computation efficient. NAIT is a lazy-learning approach with an update that is equivalent to episodic Monte-Carlo on episode completion, but that allows the stable incorporation of rewards while an episode is ongoing. We make use of a fixed domain-agnostic representation, simple distance based exploration and a proximity graph-based lookup to facilitate extremely fast execution. We empirically evaluate NAIT on both the 26 and 57 game variants of ATARI100k where, despite its simplicity, it achieves competitive performance in the online setting with greater than 100x speedup in wall-time.
summary: We present Nonparametric Approximation of Inter-Trace returns (NAIT), a Reinforcement Learning algorithm for discrete action, pixel-based environments that is both highly sample and computation efficient. NAIT is a lazy-learning approach with an update that is equivalent to episodic Monte-Carlo on episode completion, but that allows the stable incorporation of rewards while an episode is ongoing. We make use of a fixed domain-agnostic representation, simple distance based exploration and a proximity graph-based lookup to facilitate extremely fast execution. We empirically evaluate NAIT on both the 26 and 57 game variants of ATARI100k where, despite its simplicity, it achieves competitive performance in the online setting with greater than 100x speedup in wall-time.
abstract_zh: 我们提出了轨迹间返回的非参数逼近 (NAIT)，这是一种用于离散动作、基于像素的环境的强化学习算法，具有很高的样本效率和计算效率。 NAIT 是一种惰性学习方法，其更新相当于情节完成时的情节蒙特卡罗，但允许在情节正在进行时稳定地合并奖励。我们利用固定的与域无关的表示、简单的基于距离的探索和基于邻近图的查找来促进极快的执行。我们在 ATARI100k 的 26 和 57 游戏变体上对 NAIT 进行了实证评估，尽管它很简单，但它在在线环境中实现了具有竞争力的性能，并且在实际时间内加速超过 100 倍。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为NAIT的非参数值逼近强化学习算法，旨在提高像素环境下的样本和计算效率。
AI.motivation: 现有强化学习方法在像素环境中通常需要大量样本和计算资源，难以实现高效训练。
AI.method: 采用懒惰学习策略，结合固定领域无关表示、基于距离的探索及邻近图查找，实现快速执行与稳定奖励整合。
AI.result: 在ATARI100k的26和57款游戏中，NAIT在线设置下表现优异，墙时速度提升超过100倍。
AI.conclusion: NAIT凭借其简洁性与高效性，在像素环境强化学习中展现出竞争力。

authors_text: Fakrul Islam Tushar,Ehsan Abadi,Saman Sotoudeh-Paima,Rafael B. Fricks,Maciej A. Mazurowski,W. Paul Segars,Ehsan Samei,Joseph Y. Lo
title: Virtual vs. Reality: External Validation of COVID-19 Classifiers using XCAT Phantoms for Chest Computed Tomography
title_zh: 虚拟与现实：使用 XCAT Phantoms 进行胸部计算机断层扫描的 COVID-19 分类器的外部验证
comment: 7 pages, 5 figures, 2 tables, presented at the Medical Imaging 2022: Computer-Aided Diagnosis, 2022
comments: 7 pages, 5 figures, 2 tables, presented at the Medical Imaging 2022: Computer-Aided Diagnosis, 2022
abstract: Research studies of artificial intelligence models in medical imaging have been hampered by poor generalization. This problem has been especially concerning over the last year with numerous applications of deep learning for COVID-19 diagnosis. Virtual imaging trials (VITs) could provide a solution for objective evaluation of these models. In this work utilizing the VITs, we created the CVIT-COVID dataset including 180 virtually imaged computed tomography (CT) images from simulated COVID-19 and normal phantom models under different COVID-19 morphology and imaging properties. We evaluated the performance of an open-source, deep-learning model from the University of Waterloo trained with multi-institutional data and an in-house model trained with the open clinical dataset called MosMed. We further validated the model's performance against open clinical data of 305 CT images to understand virtual vs. real clinical data performance. The open-source model was published with nearly perfect performance on the original Waterloo dataset but showed a consistent performance drop in external testing on another clinical dataset (AUC=0.77) and our simulated CVIT-COVID dataset (AUC=0.55). The in-house model achieved an AUC of 0.87 while testing on the internal test set (MosMed test set). However, performance dropped to an AUC of 0.65 and 0.69 when evaluated on clinical and our simulated CVIT-COVID dataset. The VIT framework offered control over imaging conditions, allowing us to show there was no change in performance as CT exposure was changed from 28.5 to 57 mAs. The VIT framework also provided voxel-level ground truth, revealing that performance of in-house model was much higher at AUC=0.87 for diffuse COVID-19 infection size >2.65% lung volume versus AUC=0.52 for focal disease with <2.65% volume. The virtual imaging framework enabled these uniquely rigorous analyses of model performance.
summary: Research studies of artificial intelligence models in medical imaging have been hampered by poor generalization. This problem has been especially concerning over the last year with numerous applications of deep learning for COVID-19 diagnosis. Virtual imaging trials (VITs) could provide a solution for objective evaluation of these models. In this work utilizing the VITs, we created the CVIT-COVID dataset including 180 virtually imaged computed tomography (CT) images from simulated COVID-19 and normal phantom models under different COVID-19 morphology and imaging properties. We evaluated the performance of an open-source, deep-learning model from the University of Waterloo trained with multi-institutional data and an in-house model trained with the open clinical dataset called MosMed. We further validated the model's performance against open clinical data of 305 CT images to understand virtual vs. real clinical data performance. The open-source model was published with nearly perfect performance on the original Waterloo dataset but showed a consistent performance drop in external testing on another clinical dataset (AUC=0.77) and our simulated CVIT-COVID dataset (AUC=0.55). The in-house model achieved an AUC of 0.87 while testing on the internal test set (MosMed test set). However, performance dropped to an AUC of 0.65 and 0.69 when evaluated on clinical and our simulated CVIT-COVID dataset. The VIT framework offered control over imaging conditions, allowing us to show there was no change in performance as CT exposure was changed from 28.5 to 57 mAs. The VIT framework also provided voxel-level ground truth, revealing that performance of in-house model was much higher at AUC=0.87 for diffuse COVID-19 infection size >2.65% lung volume versus AUC=0.52 for focal disease with <2.65% volume. The virtual imaging framework enabled these uniquely rigorous analyses of model performance.
abstract_zh: 医学成像人工智能模型的研究因泛化能力差而受到阻碍。去年，随着深度学习在 COVID-19 诊断中的大量应用，这个问题尤其令人担忧。虚拟成像试验（VIT）可以为这些模型的客观评估提供解决方案。在这项利用 VIT 的工作中，我们创建了 CVIT-COVID 数据集，其中包括 180 个虚拟成像计算机断层扫描 (CT) 图像，这些图像来自模拟的 COVID-19 和不同 COVID-19 形态和成像特性下的正常体模模型。我们评估了滑铁卢大学使用多机构数据训练的开源深度学习模型和使用名为 MosMed 的开放临床数据集训练的内部模型的性能。我们进一步根据 305 张 CT 图像的开放临床数据验证了模型的性能，以了解虚拟与真实临床数据的性能。该开源模型在原始 Waterloo 数据集上发布时具有近乎完美的性能，但在另一个临床数据集 (AUC=0.77) 和我们的模拟 CVIT-COVID 数据集 (AUC=0.55) 的外部测试中表现出一致的性能下降。在内部测试集（MosMed 测试集）上进行测试时，内部模型的 AUC 为 0.87。然而，在临床和我们的模拟 CVIT-COVID 数据集上进行评估时，性能下降至 AUC 0.65 和 0.69。 VIT 框架提供了对成像条件的控制，使我们能够证明当 CT 曝光从 28.5 mA 更改为 57 mAs 时，性能没有变化。 VIT 框架还提供了体素水平的基本事实，表明对于弥漫性 COVID-19 感染大小 > 2.65% 肺体积的 AUC = 0.87，内部模型的性能要高得多，而对于体积 < 2.65% 的局灶性疾病， AUC = 0.52。虚拟成像框架支持对模型性能进行这些独特的严格分析。

AI.tldr: 本文通过虚拟成像试验（VITs）构建CVIT-COVID数据集，评估深度学习模型在COVID-19胸部CT分类中的泛化能力。
AI.motivation: 人工智能医学影像模型存在泛化能力差的问题，尤其在COVID-19诊断中需客观验证模型性能。
AI.method: 利用XCAT虚拟人体模型生成180张虚拟CT图像，测试开源模型（Waterloo）和自研模型（MosMed）在虚拟与真实临床数据中的表现。
AI.result: 开源模型在真实数据（AUC=0.77）和虚拟数据（AUC=0.55）中性能下降；自研模型在内部测试集（AUC=0.87）表现良好，但在真实数据（AUC=0.65）和虚拟数据（AUC=0.69）中下降。虚拟框架显示模型对弥漫性感染（AUC=0.87）优于局灶性感染（AUC=0.52）。
AI.conclusion: 虚拟成像框架能有效控制影像条件并提供像素级真值，揭示模型性能差异，为AI模型评估提供严谨方法。

search_text: Virtual vs. Reality: External Validation of COVID-19 Classifiers using XCAT Phantoms for Chest Computed Tomography 虚拟与现实：使用 XCAT Phantoms 进行胸部计算机断层扫描的 COVID-19 分类器的外部验证 Research studies of artificial intelligence models in medical imaging have been hampered by poor generalization. This problem has been especially concerning over the last year with numerous applications of deep learning for COVID-19 diagnosis. Virtual imaging trials (VITs) could provide a solution for objective evaluation of these models. In this work utilizing the VITs, we created the CVIT-COVID dataset including 180 virtually imaged computed tomography (CT) images from simulated COVID-19 and normal phantom models under different COVID-19 morphology and imaging properties. We evaluated the performance of an open-source, deep-learning model from the University of Waterloo trained with multi-institutional data and an in-house model trained with the open clinical dataset called MosMed. We further validated the model's performance against open clinical data of 305 CT images to understand virtual vs. real clinical data performance. The open-source model was published with nearly perfect performance on the original Waterloo dataset but showed a consistent performance drop in external testing on another clinical dataset (AUC=0.77) and our simulated CVIT-COVID dataset (AUC=0.55). The in-house model achieved an AUC of 0.87 while testing on the internal test set (MosMed test set). However, performance dropped to an AUC of 0.65 and 0.69 when evaluated on clinical and our simulated CVIT-COVID dataset. The VIT framework offered control over imaging conditions, allowing us to show there was no change in performance as CT exposure was changed from 28.5 to 57 mAs. The VIT framework also provided voxel-level ground truth, revealing that performance of in-house model was much higher at AUC=0.87 for diffuse COVID-19 infection size >2.65% lung volume versus AUC=0.52 for focal disease with <2.65% volume. The virtual imaging framework enabled these uniquely rigorous analyses of model performance. 医学成像人工智能模型的研究因泛化能力差而受到阻碍。去年，随着深度学习在 COVID-19 诊断中的大量应用，这个问题尤其令人担忧。虚拟成像试验（VIT）可以为这些模型的客观评估提供解决方案。在这项利用 VIT 的工作中，我们创建了 CVIT-COVID 数据集，其中包括 180 个虚拟成像计算机断层扫描 (CT) 图像，这些图像来自模拟的 COVID-19 和不同 COVID-19 形态和成像特性下的正常体模模型。我们评估了滑铁卢大学使用多机构数据训练的开源深度学习模型和使用名为 MosMed 的开放临床数据集训练的内部模型的性能。我们进一步根据 305 张 CT 图像的开放临床数据验证了模型的性能，以了解虚拟与真实临床数据的性能。该开源模型在原始 Waterloo 数据集上发布时具有近乎完美的性能，但在另一个临床数据集 (AUC=0.77) 和我们的模拟 CVIT-COVID 数据集 (AUC=0.55) 的外部测试中表现出一致的性能下降。在内部测试集（MosMed 测试集）上进行测试时，内部模型的 AUC 为 0.87。然而，在临床和我们的模拟 CVIT-COVID 数据集上进行评估时，性能下降至 AUC 0.65 和 0.69。 VIT 框架提供了对成像条件的控制，使我们能够证明当 CT 曝光从 28.5 mA 更改为 57 mAs 时，性能没有变化。 VIT 框架还提供了体素水平的基本事实，表明对于弥漫性 COVID-19 感染大小 > 2.65% 肺体积的 AUC = 0.87，内部模型的性能要高得多，而对于体积 < 2.65% 的局灶性疾病， AUC = 0.52。虚拟成像框架支持对模型性能进行这些独特的严格分析。 本文通过虚拟成像试验（VITs）构建CVIT-COVID数据集，评估深度学习模型在COVID-19胸部CT分类中的泛化能力。 Fakrul Islam Tushar,Ehsan Abadi,Saman Sotoudeh-Paima,Rafael B. authors_text: Neeraj Varshney,Swaroop Mishra,Chitta Baral
title: ILDAE: Instance-Level Difficulty Analysis of Evaluation Data
title_zh: ILDAE：评估数据实例级难度分析
abstract: Knowledge of questions' difficulty level helps a teacher in several ways, such as estimating students' potential quickly by asking carefully selected questions and improving quality of examination by modifying trivial and hard questions. Can we extract such benefits of instance difficulty in NLP? To this end, we conduct Instance-Level Difficulty Analysis of Evaluation data (ILDAE) in a large-scale setup of 23 datasets and demonstrate its five novel applications: 1) conducting efficient-yet-accurate evaluations with fewer instances saving computational cost and time, 2) improving quality of existing evaluation datasets by repairing erroneous and trivial instances, 3) selecting the best model based on application requirements, 4) analyzing dataset characteristics for guiding future data creation, 5) estimating Out-of-Domain performance reliably. Comprehensive experiments for these applications result in several interesting findings, such as evaluation using just 5% instances (selected via ILDAE) achieves as high as 0.93 Kendall correlation with evaluation using complete dataset and computing weighted accuracy using difficulty scores leads to 5.2% higher correlation with Out-of-Domain performance. We release the difficulty scores and hope our analyses and findings will bring more attention to this important yet understudied field of leveraging instance difficulty in evaluations.
summary: Knowledge of questions' difficulty level helps a teacher in several ways, such as estimating students' potential quickly by asking carefully selected questions and improving quality of examination by modifying trivial and hard questions. Can we extract such benefits of instance difficulty in NLP? To this end, we conduct Instance-Level Difficulty Analysis of Evaluation data (ILDAE) in a large-scale setup of 23 datasets and demonstrate its five novel applications: 1) conducting efficient-yet-accurate evaluations with fewer instances saving computational cost and time, 2) improving quality of existing evaluation datasets by repairing erroneous and trivial instances, 3) selecting the best model based on application requirements, 4) analyzing dataset characteristics for guiding future data creation, 5) estimating Out-of-Domain performance reliably. Comprehensive experiments for these applications result in several interesting findings, such as evaluation using just 5% instances (selected via ILDAE) achieves as high as 0.93 Kendall correlation with evaluation using complete dataset and computing weighted accuracy using difficulty scores leads to 5.2% higher correlation with Out-of-Domain performance. We release the difficulty scores and hope our analyses and findings will bring more attention to this important yet understudied field of leveraging instance difficulty in evaluations.
abstract_zh: 了解问题的难度水平可以通过多种方式帮助教师，例如通过提出精心挑选的问题来快速评估学生的潜力，以及通过修改琐碎和困难的问题来提高考试质量。我们能否从 NLP 中的实例难度中获得这样的好处？为此，我们在 23 个数据集的大规模设置中进行了评估数据的实例级难度分析 (ILDAE)，并展示了其五种新颖的应用：1）用更少的实例进行高效而准确的评估，节省计算成本和时间，2）通过修复错误和琐碎的实例来提高现有评估数据集的质量，3）根据应用程序需求选择最佳模型，4）分析数据集特征以指导未来的数据创建，5）可靠地估计域外性能。这些应用程序的综合实验得出了一些有趣的发现，例如仅使用 5% 的实例（通过 ILDAE 选择）进行评估，与使用完整数据集的评估相比，Kendall 相关性高达 0.93，而使用难度分数的计算加权精度则与域外性能的相关性提高了 5.2%。我们发布了难度分数，并希望我们的分析和发现能够引起人们对在评估中利用实例难度这一重要但尚未充分研究的领域的更多关注。

AI.tldr: 本文提出ILDAE方法，用于对自然语言处理（NLP）评估数据中的实例级难度进行分析，并展示了其在多个应用场景中的有效性。
AI.motivation: 通过分析问题难度，教师可以优化考试设计和学生评估。作者希望将这一理念引入NLP领域，以提升评估效率和数据质量。
AI.method: 在23个大规模数据集上实施实例级难度分析（ILDAE），并通过五种应用（如高效评估、数据集修复、模型选择等）验证其效果。
AI.result: 实验表明，仅使用5%的高难度实例即可达到与完整数据集0.93的Kendall相关性；结合难度评分的加权准确率使领域外性能预测提升5.2%。
AI.conclusion: ILDAE方法为NLP评估提供了实用工具，能够优化计算成本、提升数据质量，并可靠估计模型泛化能力，未来需进一步探索其应用潜力。

authors_text: Canwen Xu,Zexue He,Zhankui He,Julian McAuley
title: Leashing the Inner Demons: Self-Detoxification for Language Models
title_zh: 释放内心的恶魔：语言模型的自我解毒
abstract: Language models (LMs) can reproduce (or amplify) toxic language seen during training, which poses a risk to their practical application. In this paper, we conduct extensive experiments to study this phenomenon. We analyze the impact of prompts, decoding strategies and training corpora on the output toxicity. Based on our findings, we propose a simple yet effective method for language models to "detoxify" themselves without an additional large corpus or external discriminator. Compared to a supervised baseline, our proposed method shows better toxicity reduction with good generation quality in the generated content under multiple settings. Warning: some examples shown in the paper may contain uncensored offensive content.
summary: Language models (LMs) can reproduce (or amplify) toxic language seen during training, which poses a risk to their practical application. In this paper, we conduct extensive experiments to study this phenomenon. We analyze the impact of prompts, decoding strategies and training corpora on the output toxicity. Based on our findings, we propose a simple yet effective method for language models to "detoxify" themselves without an additional large corpus or external discriminator. Compared to a supervised baseline, our proposed method shows better toxicity reduction with good generation quality in the generated content under multiple settings. Warning: some examples shown in the paper may contain uncensored offensive content.
abstract_zh: 语言模型（LM）可以重现（或放大）训练期间看到的有毒语言，这对其实际应用构成风险。在本文中，我们进行了大量的实验来研究这种现象。我们分析了提示、解码策略和训练语料库对输出毒性的影响。根据我们的发现，我们提出了一种简单而有效的方法，让语言模型能够自我“解毒”，而无需额外的大型语料库或外部判别器。与监督基线相比，我们提出的方法在多种设置下生成的内容显示出更好的毒性降低和良好的生成质量。警告：本文中显示的一些示例可能包含未经审查的攻击性内容。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种无需额外语料或外部判别器的语言模型自我净化方法，以减少生成内容中的毒性。
AI.motivation: 语言模型可能复制或放大训练数据中的有毒语言，这对实际应用构成风险，需有效降低输出毒性。
AI.method: 通过分析提示、解码策略及训练语料对输出毒性的影响，提出基于内部机制的自我净化方法。
AI.result: 在多种设置下，该方法相比监督基线能更有效地降低毒性，同时保持生成内容的质量。
AI.conclusion: 本文方法为语言模型提供了一种简单有效的自我净化方案，有助于提升模型输出的安全性。

authors_text: Yinghui Pan,Hanyi Zhang,Yifeng Zeng,Biyang Ma,Jing Tang,Zhong Ming
title: Diversifying Agent's Behaviors in Interactive Decision Models
title_zh: 交互式决策模型中代理行为的多样化
abstract: Modelling other agents' behaviors plays an important role in decision models for interactions among multiple agents. To optimise its own decisions, a subject agent needs to model what other agents act simultaneously in an uncertain environment. However, modelling insufficiency occurs when the agents are competitive and the subject agent can not get full knowledge about other agents. Even when the agents are collaborative, they may not share their true behaviors due to their privacy concerns. In this article, we investigate into diversifying behaviors of other agents in the subject agent's decision model prior to their interactions. Starting with prior knowledge about other agents' behaviors, we use a linear reduction technique to extract representative behavioral features from the known behaviors. We subsequently generate their new behaviors by expanding the features and propose two diversity measurements to select top-K behaviors. We demonstrate the performance of the new techniques in two well-studied problem domains. This research will contribute to intelligent systems dealing with unknown unknowns in an open artificial intelligence world.
summary: Modelling other agents' behaviors plays an important role in decision models for interactions among multiple agents. To optimise its own decisions, a subject agent needs to model what other agents act simultaneously in an uncertain environment. However, modelling insufficiency occurs when the agents are competitive and the subject agent can not get full knowledge about other agents. Even when the agents are collaborative, they may not share their true behaviors due to their privacy concerns. In this article, we investigate into diversifying behaviors of other agents in the subject agent's decision model prior to their interactions. Starting with prior knowledge about other agents' behaviors, we use a linear reduction technique to extract representative behavioral features from the known behaviors. We subsequently generate their new behaviors by expanding the features and propose two diversity measurements to select top-K behaviors. We demonstrate the performance of the new techniques in two well-studied problem domains. This research will contribute to intelligent systems dealing with unknown unknowns in an open artificial intelligence world.
abstract_zh: 对其他代理的行为进行建模在多个代理之间交互的决策模型中起着重要作用。为了优化自己的决策，主体代理需要对其他代理在不确定的环境中同时采取的行动进行建模。然而，当智能体具有竞争性并且主体智能体无法充分了解其他智能体时，就会出现建模不足。即使代理进行协作，由于隐私问题，他们也可能不会分享自己的真实行为。在本文中，我们研究了主体代理决策模型中其他代理在交互之前的多样化行为。从有关其他代理行为的先验知识开始，我们使用线性缩减技术从已知行为中提取代表性行为特征。随后，我们通过扩展特征来生成它们的新行为，并提出两种多样性测量来选择前 K 行为。我们在两个经过充分研究的问题领域展示了新技术的性能。这项研究将为在开放的人工智能世界中处理未知的未知事物的智能系统做出贡献。

AI.tldr: 本文旨在通过多样化其他智能体的行为特征，提升主体智能体在不确定环境下的决策能力。
AI.motivation: 现有模型因竞争关系、隐私限制或知识不足，难以准确建模其他智能体行为，导致决策优化受限。
AI.method: 基于先验行为知识，采用线性降维提取特征并生成新行为，通过两种多样性指标筛选Top-K行为。
AI.result: 在两个经典问题领域验证了方法有效性，为应对开放AI环境中的未知不确定性提供了新思路。
AI.conclusion: 该方法通过行为多样化增强决策鲁棒性，对处理开放环境下未知未知问题具有重要价值。

authors_text: Hung Phan,Ali Jannesari
title: Story Point Effort Estimation by Text Level Graph Neural Network
title_zh: 通过文本级图神经网络进行故事点工作量估计
comment: accepted at The 1st International Workshop on Natural Language-based Software Engineering (to appear)
comments: accepted at The 1st International Workshop on Natural Language-based Software Engineering (to appear)
abstract: Estimating the software projects' efforts developed by agile methods is important for project managers or technical leads. It provides a summary as a first view of how many hours and developers are required to complete the tasks. There are research works on automatic predicting the software efforts, including Term Frequency Inverse Document Frequency (TFIDF) as the traditional approach for this problem. Graph Neural Network is a new approach that has been applied in Natural Language Processing for text classification. The advantages of Graph Neural Network are based on the ability to learn information via graph data structure, which has more representations such as the relationships between words compared to approaches of vectorizing sequence of words. In this paper, we show the potential and possible challenges of Graph Neural Network text classification in story point level estimation. By the experiments, we show that the GNN Text Level Classification can achieve as high accuracy as about 80 percent for story points level classification, which is comparable to the traditional approach. We also analyze the GNN approach and point out several current disadvantages that the GNN approach can improve for this problem or other problems in software engineering.
summary: Estimating the software projects' efforts developed by agile methods is important for project managers or technical leads. It provides a summary as a first view of how many hours and developers are required to complete the tasks. There are research works on automatic predicting the software efforts, including Term Frequency Inverse Document Frequency (TFIDF) as the traditional approach for this problem. Graph Neural Network is a new approach that has been applied in Natural Language Processing for text classification. The advantages of Graph Neural Network are based on the ability to learn information via graph data structure, which has more representations such as the relationships between words compared to approaches of vectorizing sequence of words. In this paper, we show the potential and possible challenges of Graph Neural Network text classification in story point level estimation. By the experiments, we show that the GNN Text Level Classification can achieve as high accuracy as about 80 percent for story points level classification, which is comparable to the traditional approach. We also analyze the GNN approach and point out several current disadvantages that the GNN approach can improve for this problem or other problems in software engineering.
abstract_zh: 评估通过敏捷方法开发的软件项目的工作量对于项目经理或技术主管来说非常重要。它提供了一个摘要，作为完成任务所需的时间和开发人员的第一视图。有一些关于自动预测软件工作量的研究工作，包括术语频率逆文档频率（TFIDF）作为解决此问题的传统方法。图神经网络是一种应用于自然语言处理文本分类的新方法。图神经网络的优点是基于通过图数据结构学习信息的能力，与向量化单词序列的方法相比，它具有更多的表示形式，例如单词之间的关系。在本文中，我们展示了图神经网络文本分类在故事点级别估计中的潜在和可能的挑战。通过实验，我们表明 GNN 文本级别分类对于故事点级别分类可以达到约 80% 的高精度，与传统方法相当。我们还分析了 GNN 方法，并指出了 GNN 方法目前可以​​针对这个问题或软件工程中的其他问题进行改进的几个缺点。

AI.tldr: 本文探讨使用文本级图神经网络（GNN）进行用户故事点工作量估计的方法及其挑战。
AI.motivation: 传统的TFIDF方法在软件项目工作量估计中存在局限性，而图神经网络能通过图结构捕捉词与词之间的关系，提供更丰富的文本表示，从而可能提升估计准确性。
AI.method: 基于图神经网络的文本分类方法，将用户故事文本建模为图结构，利用GNN学习节点（词）间的关系进行故事点分类。
AI.result: 实验表明，GNN在故事点分类任务中达到约80%的准确率，与传统方法相当，但存在可改进的不足。
AI.conclusion: GNN在文本级工作量估计中展现出潜力，但需进一步优化以解决当前局限性，并可拓展至其他软件工程问题。

In this paper, we show the potential and possible challenges of Graph Neural Network text classification in story point level estimation. By the experiments, we show that the GNN Text Level Classification can achieve as high accuracy as about 80 percent for story points level classification, which is comparable to the traditional approach. We also analyze the GNN approach and point out several current disadvantages that the GNN approach can improve for this problem or other problems in software engineering. 评估通过敏捷方法开发的软件项目的工作量对于项目经理或技术主管来说非常重要。它提供了一个摘要，作为完成任务所需的时间和开发人员的第一视图。有一些关于自动预测软件工作量的研究工作，包括术语频率逆文档频率（TFIDF）作为解决此问题的传统方法。图神经网络是一种应用于自然语言处理文本分类的新方法。图神经网络的优点是基于通过图数据结构学习信息的能力，与向量化单词序列的方法相比，它具有更多的表示形式，例如单词之间的关系。在本文中，我们展示了图神经网络文本分类在故事点级别估计中的潜在和可能的挑战。通过实验，我们表明 GNN 文本级别分类对于故事点级别分类可以达到约 80% 的高精度，与传统方法相当。我们还分析了 GNN 方法，并指出了 GNN 方法目前可以​​针对这个问题或软件工程中的其他问题进行改进的几个缺点。 本文探讨使用文本级图神经网络（GNN）进行用户故事点工作量估计的方法及其挑战。 Hung Phan,Ali Jannesari cs.SE,cs.AI

authors_text: Abduallah Mohamed,Deyao Zhu,Warren Vu,Mohamed Elhoseiny,Christian Claudel
title: Social-Implicit: Rethinking Trajectory Prediction Evaluation and The Effectiveness of Implicit Maximum Likelihood Estimation
title_zh: 社会隐式：重新思考轨迹预测评估和隐式最大似然估计的有效性
abstract: Best-of-N (BoN) Average Displacement Error (ADE)/ Final Displacement Error (FDE) is the most used metric for evaluating trajectory prediction models. Yet, the BoN does not quantify the whole generated samples, resulting in an incomplete view of the model's prediction quality and performance. We propose a new metric, Average Mahalanobis Distance (AMD) to tackle this issue. AMD is a metric that quantifies how close the whole generated samples are to the ground truth. We also introduce the Average Maximum Eigenvalue (AMV) metric that quantifies the overall spread of the predictions. Our metrics are validated empirically by showing that the ADE/FDE is not sensitive to distribution shifts, giving a biased sense of accuracy, unlike the AMD/AMV metrics. We introduce the usage of Implicit Maximum Likelihood Estimation (IMLE) as a replacement for traditional generative models to train our model, Social-Implicit. IMLE training mechanism aligns with AMD/AMV objective of predicting trajectories that are close to the ground truth with a tight spread. Social-Implicit is a memory efficient deep model with only 5.8K parameters that runs in real time of about 580Hz and achieves competitive results. Interactive demo of the problem can be seen at https://www.abduallahmohamed.com/social-implicit-amdamv-adefde-demo . Code is available at https://github.com/abduallahmohamed/Social-Implicit .
summary: Best-of-N (BoN) Average Displacement Error (ADE)/ Final Displacement Error (FDE) is the most used metric for evaluating trajectory prediction models. Yet, the BoN does not quantify the whole generated samples, resulting in an incomplete view of the model's prediction quality and performance. We propose a new metric, Average Mahalanobis Distance (AMD) to tackle this issue. AMD is a metric that quantifies how close the whole generated samples are to the ground truth. We also introduce the Average Maximum Eigenvalue (AMV) metric that quantifies the overall spread of the predictions. Our metrics are validated empirically by showing that the ADE/FDE is not sensitive to distribution shifts, giving a biased sense of accuracy, unlike the AMD/AMV metrics. We introduce the usage of Implicit Maximum Likelihood Estimation (IMLE) as a replacement for traditional generative models to train our model, Social-Implicit. IMLE training mechanism aligns with AMD/AMV objective of predicting trajectories that are close to the ground truth with a tight spread. Social-Implicit is a memory efficient deep model with only 5.8K parameters that runs in real time of about 580Hz and achieves competitive results. Interactive demo of the problem can be seen at https://www.abduallahmohamed.com/social-implicit-amdamv-adefde-demo . Code is available at https://github.com/abduallahmohamed/Social-Implicit .
abstract_zh: Best-of-N (BoN) 平均位移误差 (ADE)/最终位移误差 (FDE) 是评估轨迹预测模型最常用的指标。然而，BoN 并未量化整个生成的样本，导致模型的预测质量和性能的视图不完整。我们提出了一个新的指标，平均马氏距离（AMD）来解决这个问题。 AMD 是一种量化整个生成样本与真实情况的接近程度的指标。我们还引入了平均最大特征值 (AMV) 指标，用于量化预测的总体分布。我们的指标经过实证验证，表明 ADE/FDE 对分布变化不敏感，与 AMD/AMV 指标不同，给人一种准确度有偏差的感觉。我们介绍使用隐式最大似然估计（IMLE）作为传统生成模型的替代品来训练我们的模型“社会隐式”。 IMLE 训练机制与 AMD/AMV 预测轨迹的目标一致，该轨迹接近真实情况且分布紧密。 Social-Implicit 是一种内存高效的深度模型，只有 5.8K 参数，在约 580Hz 的实时运行速度并取得了有竞争力的结果。该问题的交互式演示可以在 https://www.abduallahmohamed.com/social-implicit-amdamv-adefde-demo 看到。代码可在 https://github.com/abduallahmohamed/Social-Implicit 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种新的轨迹预测评估指标（AMD/AMV）和基于隐式最大似然估计（IMLE）的高效模型Social-Implicit，以改进传统评估方法的不足。
AI.motivation: 传统Best-of-N（BoN）指标（如ADE/FDE）仅评估部分生成样本，无法全面反映模型预测质量，且对分布偏移不敏感，导致评估偏差。
AI.method: 提出Average Mahalanobis Distance（AMD）量化样本与真实值的接近程度，Average Maximum Eigenvalue（AMV）衡量预测分布的离散度；采用IMLE训练机制替代传统生成模型，构建参数量仅5.8K的Social-Implicit模型。
AI.result: Social-Implicit在保持实时性（580Hz）和低参数量的同时，取得具有竞争力的预测效果；实验验证AMD/AMV比ADE/FDE更敏感于分布偏移。
AI.conclusion: 新指标与IMLE框架共同提升了轨迹预测的评估全面性与模型效率，为未来研究提供了更可靠的评估基准和实现方案。

authors_text: Chen Tang,Frank Guerin,Chenghua Lin
title: Recent Advances in Neural Text Generation: A Task-Agnostic Survey
title_zh: 神经文本生成的最新进展：与任务无关的调查
comment: This has been updated with some recent advances in 2023
comments: This has been updated with some recent advances in 2023
abstract: In recent years, considerable research has been dedicated to the application of neural models in the field of natural language generation (NLG). The primary objective is to generate text that is both linguistically natural and human-like, while also exerting control over the generation process. This paper offers a comprehensive and task-agnostic survey of the recent advancements in neural text generation. These advancements have been facilitated through a multitude of developments, which we categorize into four key areas: data construction, neural frameworks, training and inference strategies, and evaluation metrics. By examining these different aspects, we aim to provide a holistic overview of the progress made in the field. Furthermore, we explore the future directions for the advancement of neural text generation, which encompass the utilization of neural pipelines and the incorporation of background knowledge. These avenues present promising opportunities to further enhance the capabilities of NLG systems. Overall, this survey serves to consolidate the current state of the art in neural text generation and highlights potential avenues for future research and development in this dynamic field.

The primary objective is to generate text that is both linguistically natural and human-like, while also exerting control over the generation process. This paper offers a comprehensive and task-agnostic survey of the recent advancements in neural text generation. These advancements have been facilitated through a multitude of developments, which we categorize into four key areas: data construction, neural frameworks, training and inference strategies, and evaluation metrics. By examining these different aspects, we aim to provide a holistic overview of the progress made in the field. Furthermore, we explore the future directions for the advancement of neural text generation, which encompass the utilization of neural pipelines and the incorporation of background knowledge. These avenues present promising opportunities to further enhance the capabilities of NLG systems. Overall, this survey serves to consolidate the current state of the art in neural text generation and highlights potential avenues for future research and development in this dynamic field.
abstract_zh: 近年来，大量研究致力于神经模型在自然语言生成（NLG）领域的应用。主要目标是生成语言上自然且类似人类的文本，同时对生成过程进行控制。本文对神经文本生成的最新进展进行了全面且与任务无关的调查。这些进步是通过大量的发展得到促进的，我们将其分为四个关键领域：数据构建、神经框架、训练和推理策略以及评估指标。通过研究这些不同的方面，我们的目标是提供该领域所取得进展的整体概述。此外，我们探索了神经文本生成的未来发展方向，其中包括神经管道的利用和背景知识的结合。这些途径为进一步增强 NLG 系统的能力提供了有希望的机会。总体而言，这项调查旨在巩固神经文本生成领域的最新技术，并强调这一动态领域未来研究和开发的潜在途径。

AI.tldr: 本文是一篇关于神经文本生成领域的全面综述，总结了不特定任务的最新进展。
AI.motivation: 尽管神经文本生成研究迅速发展，但缺乏系统性、任务无关的综述来整合现状并指导未来方向。
AI.method: 将技术进展分为数据构建、神经框架、训练推理策略和评估指标四个维度进行分类分析，并探讨未来研究方向。
AI.result: 梳理了当前神经文本生成的技术全景，明确了未来需突破的神经流水线构建与背景知识融合等关键问题。
AI.conclusion: 该综述为NLG领域提供了系统性参考，强调通过多模态知识整合与生成控制机制提升文本生成质量。
